summary: Gruber explores a workaround for the iPhone's all-or-nothing MobileMe calendar syncing, which forces all iCal calendars onto the iPhone with no way to exclude some. He discovers that Google Calendar's newly enabled CalDAV support works well with iCal on Leopard, allowing full read-write access. However, CalDAV calendars don't sync to MobileMe, which he notes is ironic since Apple itself is a leading CalDAV proponent and uses it internally. He suggests this is likely a feature Apple hasn't built yet, and in the meantime, CalDAV calendars can serve as a useful workaround for keeping certain calendars off the iPhone.
key_points: MobileMe calendar syncing is all-or-nothing — there's no way to exclude specific iCal calendars from appearing on the iPhone
Google Calendar's new CalDAV support provides full read-write calendar access through iCal, unlike read-only ICS subscriptions
CalDAV calendars cannot sync to MobileMe, which is ironic given Apple is a leading CalDAV proponent and uses it internally via iCal Server
The CalDAV limitation means Apple's own enterprise calendars can't sync bidirectionally with the iPhone
BusySync offers a $25 workaround for users who want Google Calendar events to appear as normal local calendars in iCal
money_quotes: When you turn on MobileMe calendar syncing on your iPhone, all your local iCal calendars appear on your iPhone.
Unlike with ICS calendar subscriptions, which are read-only, iCal offers full read-write support for CalDAV calendars.
Apple is a leading proponent of the CalDAV standard.
Neither Apple's own calendars nor those of Mac OS X Server customers can sync in both directions to the iPhone. Awkward.
My guess is that this is simply a feature Apple hasn't gotten to yet, and that full CalDAV support will eventually make it to the iPhone.
key_insight: Apple's failure to support its own CalDAV standard on the iPhone creates an ironic gap where the company's enterprise calendars can't fully sync with its flagship device, but that same gap can be exploited as a workaround for selective calendar syncing.
